question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findByEvmAddress() uses a positional parameter (?1) in a native query to submit a byte array that, due to JDBC driver behavior with binary data, matches multiple entity rows, causing the Optional<Long> return to silently return only the first match and misattribute transactions to the wrong entity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract update transaction with a migration sidecar containing duplicate entity IDs, causing updateContractType() to process the same ID multiple times within the same @Transactional boundary, and through the HashSet deduplication in SidecarContractMigration potentially causing a partial batch flush that leaves entity and entity_history in inconsistent states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user craft a contract that emits synthetic transfer logs with topic1/topic2 values that decode to entity IDs of non-contract entities (accounts, tokens), causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to return those entities' EVM addresses and SyntheticLogListener to overwrite the log topics with those addresses, reorganizing the historical record of which addresses participated in the transfer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the EntityIdServiceImpl cache's behavior where notify() is called with deleted=null (which passes the 'entity.getDeleted() != null && entity.getDeleted()' check) to insert a cache entry for a logically-deleted entity, causing findByAlias() database results to be bypassed and transactions to be attributed to the deleted entity's ID?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractCreate transaction that creates a contract at an EVM address that was previously used by a deleted account, causing findByEvmAddress() to return the new contract's entity ID for that address, and thereby cause all historical transactions that referenced the old account's EVM address to be reinterpreted as referencing the new contract?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user submit a migration sidecar record during a ContractUpdate transaction that references entity IDs of accounts that have pending CryptoDelete transactions in the same record file, causing updateContractType() to reclassify those accounts as CONTRACT before the delete is processed, resulting in entity_history records showing the entity as CONTRACT type at the time of deletion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the SyntheticLogListener's updateTopicField() fallback behavior (which caches the defaultValue when no EVM address is found) to permanently cache a long-zero address for an entity that later gets an EVM address assigned, causing all future synthetic log topics referencing that entity to use the stale long-zero address instead of the real EVM address?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged user submit a CryptoTransfer transaction using an alias that resolves via findByAlias() to entity A, while simultaneously submitting a CryptoUpdate that changes entity A's alias, causing a race condition where the transfer is recorded in transaction history as involving entity A but the alias now points to entity B?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged user submit an EthereumTransaction with a 'to' field set to a long-zero EVM address (shard=0, realm=0, num=X) that bypasses findByEvmAddress() entirely via DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress(), causing the transaction to be attributed to entity X without any database lookup, and if entity X is a system account, reorganizing the transaction history to show system account involvement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that updateContractType() uses ':ids' named parameter binding (Spring Data JPA collection binding) with an Iterable<Long> to submit a migration sidecar with a very large number of entity IDs, causing the generated SQL IN clause to exceed database limits and trigger an error that partially commits the type update, leaving entity and entity_history in inconsistent states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user create a contract with an EVM address that is a prefix-padded version of another entity's long-zero address, causing findEvmAddressesByIds() to return this address and SyntheticLogListener.trim() to produce a trimmed address that collides with the other entity's canonical address, corrupting the bloom filter and topic history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged user submit a CryptoCreate with an alias that is exactly 20 bytes (EVM address length), causing EntityIdServiceImpl to route the lookup through findByEvmAddress() instead of findByAlias(), and if the EVM address matches an existing contract, cause the new account's transactions to be attributed to the contract's entity ID in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Transactional class-level annotation on EntityRepository (which defaults to REQUIRED propagation) to cause findByEvmAddress() to participate in an outer transaction that is later rolled back, leaving the in-memory EntityIdServiceImpl cache with an entity ID that no longer exists in the database, causing subsequent transactions to be attributed to a phantom entity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractUpdate transaction with a migration sidecar that references the entity ID of a SCHEDULE entity, causing updateContractType() to change the schedule's type to CONTRACT, which would cause all historical ScheduleCreate and ScheduleSign transactions involving that entity to be misclassified as contract interactions in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findEvmAddressesByIds(Iterable<? extends Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call that generates a synthetic log with a topic1 value that decodes to a long-zero entity ID (via fromTrimmedEvmAddress()), causing SyntheticLogListener to add that entity ID to the evmAddressLookupIds set and call findEvmAddressesByIds(), which then returns the entity's real EVM address and overwrites the topic1 value, reorganizing the historical log record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findByAlias() returns only the entity ID (Optional<Long>) rather than the full entity, causing EntityIdServiceImpl to create an EntityId from the raw Long without validating the entity's type, and thereby cause a TOKEN entity's ID to be used as the target of a CryptoTransfer, reorganizing the transaction history to show token entity involvement in crypto transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[] evmAddress)] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractCreate transaction where the new contract's EVM address matches an existing account's alias bytes (not EVM address), causing findByEvmAddress() to return the contract's entity ID when the account's alias is used as an EVM address lookup, creating a cross-type entity resolution collision in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateContractType(Iterable<Long> ids)] Can an unprivileged user submit a migration sidecar record with a ContractBytecode whose contractId has shard/realm values that differ from the mirror node's configured shard/realm, causing EntityId.of(contractBytecode.getContractId()).getId() to compute a different entity ID than expected and updateContractType() to reclassify the wrong entity, corrupting the transaction history for that entity?
